behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff (payload assembly + API call wrapper), not model judgment. An in-process fake API client that captures the outgoing request is sufficient to catch missing/partial taxonomy fields. Rejected System because behavior text is about request content ("includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels"), not real network/OS effects. Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model output quality, only outbound payload wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client, invoke classify(), and assert the captured request contains every configured taxonomy label with no omissions.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is response-handoff validation across module boundaries: API adapter returns a value, classifier validation layer maps out-of-taxonomy to "unknown". A stubbed API response reproduces the real failure. Rejected Agentic because behavior text targets guardrail logic ("returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding") rather than model decision quality. Rejected System because no real network/OS behavior is required to trigger the condition.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API that returns an invalid label (e.g., "medical"); call classify() and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior across repeated invocations; a deterministic stub would always pass and hide non-determinism/drift. This directly tests model decision stability for identical input, which is LLM-dependent. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real external AI classification API multiple times with the same document under fixed settings; assert label consistency rate meets the acceptance threshold (e.g., 100% or defined tolerance).

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is about actual tokens consumed by the external AI call; mocks can hide provider-side tokenization/accounting differences. Even with deterministic assertions, execution passes through a real AI API and cost outcome depends on real model/tokenizer behavior. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires real consumption ("tokens consumed ... stays within ... budget"), not just local budgeting logic. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic step is required per check.
behaviors.test_description: Run real classification calls on representative documents and assert provider-reported token usage per call does not exceed configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry orchestration logic across classifier + API client + backoff/timer components. A controlled stub can emit rate-limit then success to expose real retry-state bugs. Rejected System because real network is not required to reproduce the specific failure mode (handling a rate-limit signal). Rejected Agentic because correctness is about infrastructure behavior (retry/wait), not model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Configure stub API to return rate-limit on first call and success on second; assert classifier waits/backs off, retries, and returns successful label instead of bubbling an error.
